title: The Rent Rule Every Guru Quotes Is From 1969
hook: Everyone tells you to spend 30% of your income on rent — but that rule was created for $8,400 annual salaries, and blindly following it today could be your biggest financial mistake.
angle: Exposes the '30% on rent' rule as a 1969 HUD housing assistance threshold never meant as universal financial advice — and shows why savings rate matters more than rent ratio
audience: Renters, millennials, and anyone following the 30% rule without knowing it was written for 1969 salaries
key_numbers: 1969: rule created for $8,400 median annual income
Today: following the rule requires $82,000/year income
52% of US renters are already cost-burdened
